PARAM.description: the character to recognize as a separator
PARAM.name: separator
PARAM.content: the character to recognize as a separator

RETURN.content: splitter, with default settings, that recognizes that separator
RETURN.description: splitter, with default settings, that recognizes that separator

PARAM.description: a CharMatcher that determines whether a character is a separator
PARAM.name: separatorMatcher
PARAM.content: a CharMatcher that determines whether a character is a separator

RETURN.description: Returns a splitter that considers any single character matched by the given CharMatcher to be a separator.
RETURN.content: Returns a splitter that considers any single character matched by the given CharMatcher to be a separator.

PARAM.description: the literal, nonempty string to recognize as a separator
PARAM.name: separator
PARAM.content: the literal, nonempty string to recognize as a separator

RETURN.description: Returns a splitter that uses the given fixed string as a separator.
RETURN.content: Returns a splitter that uses the given fixed string as a separator.

PARAM.description: the pattern that determines whether a subsequence is a separator, not null
PARAM.name: separatorPattern
PARAM.content: the pattern that determines whether a subsequence is a separator

RETURN.description: Returns a splitter that considers any subsequence matching pattern to be a separator.
RETURN.content: Returns a splitter that considers any subsequence matching pattern to be a separator.

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if {@code separatorPattern} matches the empty string
THROWS.description: if {@code separatorPattern} matches the empty string

PARAM.description: the pattern that determines whether a subsequence is a separator, not null
PARAM.name: separatorPattern
PARAM.content: the pattern that determines whether a subsequence is a separator, not null

RETURN.description: Returns a splitter that considers any subsequence matching a given pattern (regular expression) to be a separator.
RETURN.content: Returns a splitter that considers any subsequence matching a given pattern (regular expression) to be a separator.

THROWS.name: java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException
THROWS.content: if {@code separatorPattern} is a malformed expression
THROWS.description: if {@code separatorPattern} is a malformed expression

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if {@code separatorPattern} matches the empty string
THROWS.description: if {@code separatorPattern} matches the empty string

PARAM.name: length
PARAM.content: the desired length of pieces after splitting, a positive integer

RETURN.description: Returns a splitter that divides strings into pieces of the given length.
RETURN.content: Returns a splitter that divides strings into pieces of the given length.

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if {@code length} is zero or negative
THROWS.description: if {@code length} is zero or negative

RETURN.description: a splitter with the desired configuration
RETURN.content: a splitter with the desired configuration

PARAM.description: the maximum number of items returned
PARAM.name: limit
PARAM.content: the maximum number of items returned

RETURN.description: Returns a splitter that behaves equivalently to this splitter but stops splitting after it reaches the limit.
RETURN.content: Returns a splitter that behaves equivalently to this splitter but stops splitting after it reaches the limit.

RETURN.description: a splitter with the desired configuration
RETURN.content: a splitter with the desired configuration

PARAM.description: a CharMatcher that determines whether a character should be removed from the beginning/end of a subsequence, not null
PARAM.name: trimmer
PARAM.content: a CharMatcher that determines whether a character should be removed from the beginning/end of a subsequence, not null

RETURN.description: Returns a splitter that behaves equivalently to this splitter, but removes all leading or trailing characters matching the given CharMatcher from each returned substring.
RETURN.content: Returns a splitter that behaves equivalently to this splitter, but removes all leading or trailing characters matching the given CharMatcher from each returned substring.

PARAM.description: the sequence of characters to split
PARAM.name: sequence
PARAM.content: the sequence of characters to split

RETURN.description: an iteration over the segments split from the parameter.
RETURN.content: an iteration over the segments split from the parameter.

PARAM.description: the sequence of characters to split
PARAM.name: sequence
PARAM.content: the sequence of characters to split

RETURN.description: an immutable list of the segments split from the parameter
